proposals.evidence: Appears in ¶3, discussing division of marital assets. 'Marital estate' is the legal term for community property; 'martial' is not a legal term. Context and all surrounding usages confirm 'marital' is correct.

proposals.evidence: Citation to Panther Pressure Testers, Inc. v. Szostak, 2023 ND 27, ¶ 8. The reporter citation 'N.W.2d' should not be followed by a period when followed by a space and page number per Bluebook format and consistent with all other reporter citations in the opinion.

proposals.source: PDF source pdftotext extraction confirms 'carryover' as one word (line 159 of source shows 'capital loss carryover')
proposals.evidence: Appears in ¶6 in sentence 'Renee Anderson's proposed findings and proposed distribution of property did not include any reference to the capital loss carryover.' The single-word form 'carryover' is the correct legal and tax terminology, and is used correctly in all other 12+ instances throughout the opinion.

coverage_note: Complete opinion scanned. Three transcription/OCR defects identified and verified against PDF source. All citations, party names, dollar amounts, dates, and case numbers verified as matching source text. No missing text, no paragraph sequence errors, no heading anomalies detected. The opinion is substantially clean aside from the three identified errors."

proposals.old_exact: a When a the noncompensable organization shall award benefits on an aggravation basis, on the following terms: compensable injury, disease, or other condition, combines with injury
proposals.new_exact: When a compensable injury combines with a noncompensable injury, disease, or other condition, the organization shall award benefits on an aggravation basis, on the following terms:
proposals.evidence: DB text has severely scrambled word order in statute quotation (paragraph [¶14]). The phrase begins with 'a When a the noncompensable' and the words are transposed. Source PDF shows correct order: 'When a compensable injury combines with a noncompensable injury, disease, or other condition, the organization shall award benefits on an aggravation basis, on the following terms:' This is an OCR/ingestion defect where the statute text was reconstructed in wrong order.
proposals.note: Critical fix: statute text is fundamental to the opinion's analysis of N.D.C.C. § 65-05-15 aggravation basis requirements. The scrambled text in DB would confuse readers about the statute's actual language.

proposals.old_exact: This Court remanded this case after concluding the district court did not in make specific circumstances adversely affected the children or resulted in a general decline in the children's condition. Goetz v. Goetz, 2023 ND 53, ¶ 1, 988 N.W.2d 553. findings regarding whether the material change
proposals.new_exact: This Court remanded this case after concluding the district court did not make specific findings regarding whether the material change in circumstances adversely affected the children or resulted in a general decline in the children's condition. Goetz v. Goetz, 2023 ND 53, ¶ 1, 988 N.W.2d 553.
proposals.source: PDF source /Users/jerod/refs/nd/opin/pdfs/2023/2023ND120.pdf lines 180-183 (pdftotext output); opinion paragraph [¶14]
proposals.evidence: The DB text in paragraph 14 contains a severe text-ordering error: 'did not in make specific circumstances adversely affected the children or resulted in a general decline in the children's condition. Goetz v. Goetz, 2023 ND 53, ¶ 1, 988 N.W.2d 553. findings regarding whether the material change' is corrupted word sequencing. The PDF source clearly shows the correct order: 'did not make specific findings regarding whether the material change in circumstances adversely affected the children or resulted in a general decline in the children's condition. Goetz v. Goetz, 2023 ND 53, ¶ 1, 988 N.W.2d 553.' Confirmed by extracting all words from both versions (after the fix, word counts match exactly at 1706 words and all words appear in the same order).
proposals.note: Paragraph [¶14] begins the dissenting opinion. This scrambling is an OCR/ingestion defect. All other paragraphs (¶1-¶13 and ¶15-¶17) have been verified and contain no word-order errors.

coverage_note: Complete opinion coverage: all 17 paragraphs reviewed. Comparison method: (1) extracted opinion text (pdftotext PDF layout rendering); (2) normalized DB and source texts by removing whitespace, quote-type variations (straight vs curly), and page numbers; (3) extracted word sequences from both versions; (4) verified no differences exist after the identified fix. Single high-confidence split_join error located in paragraph 14 of dissent; all other opinion text is correct."

Full text comparison completed. Opinion verified against authoritative PDF source (2023ND60.pdf). All content elements verified present and accurate. Whitespace/line-break differences and quote character normalization (straight vs. curly quotes) are per policy and not flagged.

Complete verification against authoritative PDF source (2023ND56.pdf). Compared: case header, all 9 paragraphs, attorney listings, judge signatures, all statutory citations, case citations, reporter cites. Confirmed fidelity of transcription/OCR ingestion. Page furniture and quote-style differences identified as intentional/expected.

Full text verification completed: 1,316 normalized characters compared between database and authoritative PDF source (2023ND52.pdf). All differences accounted for: (1) straight quotes in DB vs. curly quotes in PDF = intentional policy difference; (2) page number footer ' 1' = page furniture ignored per Rule 8; (3) line breaks and spacing = layout only, not content differences.

coverage_note: Comprehensive byte-level verification: DB text compared against authoritative PDF source after normalization of UTF-8 typographic characters (curly quotes, em/en dashes, ellipsis). Opinion body (from [¶1] through justice signatures) confirmed identical in content. Full opinion verified against PDF source (pdftotext -layout). Confirmed: all paragraph markers [¶1]–[¶4], all citations (Fines, Desert Partners, Hunter), all justices signatures, all case parties, docket number, court of origin, judgment. Zero OCR errors, zero missing text. Quote character style (straight ASCII in DB vs. typographic in PDF) is intentional per policy and NOT flagged."

proposals.source: Official PDF source text (2023ND57.pdf) shows correct form: 'Wilder, 2018 ND 93, is misplaced'
proposals.evidence: The DB text has irregular line breaks after the citation ('2018 ND 93' on its own line, comma on next line), combined with incorrect verb form 'are' (plural) instead of 'is' (singular). The PDF shows the proper citation format (all on one line with comma) and the grammatically correct singular verb 'is' that agrees with the subject 'Linner's reliance' (singular noun phrase).
proposals.note: [¶22]: The citation should be formatted as a complete unit without intervening line breaks, and the subject-verb agreement requires 'is' not 'are'.

coverage_note: Comprehensive analysis: 2023 ND 67 (Whetsel v. State) - reviewed entire opinion text, verified all citations (N.D.C.C. § 29-32.1-01(3), N.D.R.App.P. 35.1(a)(6), Atkins v. State 2017 ND 290, 904 N.W.2d 738), verified party names (Byron Whetsel, State of North Dakota), verified all dates (August 4, 2022; 2023-03-31), verified all justice signatures (Jon J. Jensen C.J., Daniel J. Crothers, Lisa Fair McEvers, Jerod E. Tufte, Douglas A. Bahr), verified all numbers and special characters. Only difference from PDF source is layout artifact (line break in statute citation '29-32.1-\n01(3)' in PDF vs. correctly joined '29-32.1-01(3)' in DB) - this is correct ingestion, not an error. No OCR character confusions (l/1/I, O/0, rn/m, etc.) detected. Straight quotes vs. curly quotes per policy intentional. Four 3-line blank-runs in DB vs. two 5-line blank-runs in PDF source are structural layout differences, not content errors."

Complete opinion verified: caption, substantive paragraphs [¶1]-[¶3], judge signature line. All key elements confirmed present and accurate. Text is faithful to authoritative PDF source.

Comprehensive transcription audit of State v. Isaak, 2023 ND 44 (DB id 18156). Opinion body (from FILED through signature block) compared token-by-token against authoritative PDF source (pdftotext extraction). All 2,653 tokens match perfectly. All 15 paragraph markers verified. All section dividers, case citations, and pinpoint references verified intact. No OCR character confusions, ligature artifacts, split/joined words, or missing content detected. Form-feed characters (0x0C) in PDF source are expected pdftotext page break artifacts. Quote character style differences (PDF uses U+2019 smart quote in possessives; DB uses straight ASCII apostrophe) are intentional per database policy and not flagged. Conclusion: Opinion is suitable for adoption as authoritative digital edition."
